mission.title: argo-rollouts: Ability to add ephemeral labels to canary/stable pods
mission.description: Ability to add ephemeral labels to canary/stable pods. Requested by 19+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo-rollouts deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo-rollouts version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
helm list -n argo-rollouts 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo-rollouts installation.

steps.title: Review argo-rollouts configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-rollouts configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl get configmap -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-rollouts
```
A common request/use case is for a Rollout to label the canary/stable pods with a user-defined label, for the duration that they are the canary/stable set, and for the labels to be removed from the pods afterwards.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Ability to add ephemeral labels to canary/stable pods
steps.description:
Adds support for setting ephemeral metadata (annotations/labels) which get updated on the Pods/ReplicaSets during an update. Example syntax:

```yaml
spec:
  strategy:
    canary:
      stableMetadata:
        labels:
          role: stable
      canaryMetadata:
        labels:
          role:
```yaml
spec:
  strategy:
    canary:
      stableMetadata:
        labels:
          role: stable
      canaryMetadata:
        labels:
          role: canary
      steps:
      - setWeight: 50
      - pause: {}
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl get events -n argo-rollouts --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Ability to add ephemeral labels to canary/stable pods" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Adds support for setting ephemeral metadata (annotations/labels) which get updated on the Pods/ReplicaSets during an update. Example syntax:

```yaml
spec:
  strategy:
    canary:
      stableMetadata:
        labels:
          role: stable
      canaryMetadata:
        labels:
          role: canary
      steps:
      - setWeight: 50
      - pause: {}
```
codeSnippets:
spec:
  strategy:
    canary:
      stableMetadata:
        labels:
          role: stable
      canaryMetadata:
        labels:
          role: canary
      steps:
      - setWeight: 50
      - pause: {}

apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: guestbook-abc1234-xs2d
  labels:
    role: canary    # this label is user-defined

apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: guestbook-abc1234-xs2d
  labels:
    role: stable    # changed from role: canary since it is no longer canary after the update

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-rollouts installed or the issue environment reproducible.
